Profile

01 · Background
RoleOps & Executive Support VA
BasedCagayan de Oro, PH
TimezoneGMT+8, flexible to US
AvailabilityFull-time, Mon–Fri
RateFrom $10/hr
AI FluencyClaude, daily use

I started in customer service, where I learned how a business actually runs — one call, one order, one client at a time. That grew into operations work: building the CRM, the calendars, the inventory logs, and the follow-up sequences most business owners never have time to build for themselves.

Most recently, I ran daily operations for two US companies at once — from the same desk, the same day.

That meant designing the Notion databases both teams now run on, writing the cold email sequences that kept their pipelines fed, and giving the founder a clear read on what needed attention each week. I use Claude the way an assistant should: not to replace judgment, but to move faster through the parts of the day that don't need it.

Systems built

02 · Proof of work
CRM · Lead Pipeline

Shared CRM, two companies, one pipeline

Built and maintained a Notion CRM tracking leads for both companies at once — contact details, service interest, industry, and outreach sequence stage, all in one live view.

CRM · Contact Database

Contact records built for daily follow-up

A second CRM view organized by contact type and last-interaction date, used every day to plan who to reach out to next and when.

Operations · Inventory

Inventory tracking across multiple sites

Tracked physical inventory across delivery locations — status, batch numbers, fill and delivery dates — so nothing went missing or sat unnoticed.

Operations · Scheduling

A team schedule that replaced text threads

Built the shared schedule used to coordinate staff across roles and locations — one source of truth instead of scattered messages.

Self-Management · Ops Dashboard

The same systems thinking, applied to my own growth

A live task board I run for myself — priorities, upskilling goals, and deadlines — the same structure I bring to a founder's business, applied to my own.
